stemHtml: Whilst flying a single engine piston aircraft, you suspect carburettor icing is causing your engine to run rough, what is the correct action to take?

options.a: Turn off the carburettor heat to prevent ice from entering the engine.
options.b: Apply intermittent bursts of carburettor heat to avoid thermal shock in the cylinders.
options.c: Apply full carburettor heat and leave it on until all signs of carburettor icing have disappeared.
options.d: Apply 50% carburettor heat and reduce the amount if engine roughness increases.
